title: racosperma subflexuosum subsp. capillatum
intro: Racosperma subflexuosum subsp. capillatum, also known as the Mallee Acacia or Acacia subflexuosa, is a plant species of the genus Acacia, which belongs to the family Fabaceae. It is a shrub native to Western Australia, known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Aboriginal medicine. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, colds, and skin conditions, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Medicinal preparations include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
